question: Bundle surface: a --bundle flag on bench serve (no new verb, README surface stable), or a dedicated verb?
author: claude/design

question: Saved-view storage: browser localStorage with the URL as the canonical shareable form (server stays structurally read-only), or server-side view files?
author: claude/design

question: Filter grammar scope v1: facet fields + negation + wildcards on id-like fields + free text, or additionally comparison operators on numeric fields (cost, wall)?
author: claude/design

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive ('Begin implementing in the sequence you recommend'). The bundle is the operator view in archive form, so it lives on the operator verb — one surface to document, and the flag's presence makes 'snapshot of what serve shows' literal.
author: jyang

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive. Server-side view files would add the first observer write path; localStorage keeps the server structurally read-only and the URL stays the portable, shareable truth — a saved view IS a stored URL.
author: jyang

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive. v1 stays a closed grammar over the facets the chips already expose (plus negation, id wildcards, free text) so grammar and chips are provably two views of one state; numeric comparisons widen the state space beyond what chips can render and wait for demand.
author: jyang
